No Poverty.reason: The course teaches "socioeconomic and political statistics," crucial for measuring poverty (Target 1.2) and informing pro-poor policy frameworks (Target 1.b).

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to hunger, food security, agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a PhD/MA seminar, directly providing quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and developing "independent research ability" (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to affordable and clean energy or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course teaches "socioeconomic and political statistics," vital for analyzing economic productivity (Target 8.2) and informing development-oriented policies (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course directly enhances scientific research capabilities and increases qualified researchers (PhD/MA students), aligning explicitly with Target 9.5 on innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course provides tools like "socioeconomic and political statistics" and "opinion surveys," essential for measuring and analyzing social and economic inclusion and inequality (Targets 10.1, 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to sustainable cities, communities, or urban planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to responsible consumption, production, or resource management.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to climate action, resilience, or climate change education.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to marine life, ocean conservation, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course teaches "political statistics" and "policy experiments," key methods for developing and evaluating effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Targets 16.6, 16.7).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds capacity in "statistics" and "surveys," essential for increasing the availability of high-quality, reliable data for sustainable development (Target 17.18).
